items.title: N. Sembilan polls: Pakatan manifesto focuses on deliverable promises, says Aminuddin
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 21 — Pakatan Harapan’s (PH) manifesto for the upcoming Negeri Sembilan state election focuses on deliverable promises rather than political rhetoric, said state coalition chairman Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun. Aminuddin, who is also the Menteri Besar, said the manifesto was formulated based on PH’s eight years in office in the state, with an emphasis on continuing existing initiatives while enhancing them over the next five years. Earlier, Aminuddin, who is contesting the Linggi state seat in the election, launched the coalition manifesto themed “Negeri Sembilan Kekal Harapan” (Negeri Sembilan Stays With Harapan), which outlines 10 key commitments.

items.title: Pakatan promises jobs, homes and stronger welfare in N. Sembilan manifesto
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 21 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) yesterday unveiled its manifesto, themed “Negeri Sembilan Kekal Harapan” (Negeri Sembilan Stays With Harapan), outlining 10 key commitments as its pledge to voters for the upcoming 16th Negeri Sembilan state election. The manifesto was launched by Negeri Sembilan PH chairman and Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun here yesterday. “At the heart of this corridor stands Negeri Sembilan.

items.title: Tate brothers to fight 'politically motivated' UK extradition
items.summary: MIAMI: Influencer Andrew Tate and his brother Tristan will fight extradition to Britain on sex crime charges, their lawyer said Monday, slamming their recent arrest in Miami as "politically motivated." US authorities detained the self-professed misogynist and his younger sibling on Saturday as UK prosecutors announced new charges of rape, sex trafficking and assault. The Tate brothers now face 59 charges in total – 42 against Andrew and 17 against Tristan.

items.title: Economists: Diesel subsidy reform is working, focus now on the savings [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's targeted diesel subsidy reform is beginning to generate the fiscal savings policymakers had hoped for, but economists say the government's next challenge is ensuring those savings are put to productive use. While the mechanism is expected to gradually curb subsidy leakages and normalise diesel consumption over the coming months, they said the bigger test of the reform will be whether it creates lasting economic benefits through better public spending. Malaysia's diesel subsidy bill surged from RM1.4 billion in 2019 to RM14.3 billion in 2023 before the targeted subsidy programme was introduced.

items.title: N. Sembilan polls: MCMC activates centre to track telecoms issues
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 21 — The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) has activated its Network Monitoring Centre to oversee complaints involving communications and multimedia services during the 16th Negeri Sembilan state election. According to MCMC, the Network Monitoring Centre will also receive complaints on other matters that fall within the commission’s jurisdiction. It said public feedback would help the commission maintain the quality of communications and multimedia services throughout the state election period.

items.title: Economists: Malaysia's trade surge reflects real demand, not just base effect
items.summary: Total trade jumped 44.7 per cent year-on-year to RM340.9 billion, with exports rising 45.4 per cent to RM177.9 billion and imports increasing 43.9 per cent to RM163 billion. The performance extended Malaysia's trade surplus streak to 74 consecutive months, with the surplus expanding 64.9 per cent to RM14.9 billion. They said resilient global technology demand, together with continued investment in artificial intelligence (AI), cloud computing and data centres, had positioned Malaysia to benefit from the ongoing recovery in global trade.

items.title: Anwar to join Pakatan’s N. Sembilan campaign on Wednesday, says Aminuddin
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 21 — Prime Minister and Pakatan Harapan (PH) Chairman Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim is expected to join the campaign trail for the 16th Negeri Sembilan state election this Wednesday. PH Negeri Sembilan chairman Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun said Anwar is scheduled to join several PH campaign events in Sikamat and Paroi, alongside party leadership and election machinery. He said Anwar’s absence from last night’s PH Negeri Sembilan manifesto launch is not an issue.

items.title: 4 US service members killed in deadliest Iran-linked attacks on US forces
items.summary: RIYADH: Yemen's Iran-aligned Houthis said on Monday they would impose a naval blockade on Saudi Arabia, opening a potential new front against the United States in its war with Iran and raising the threat to global energy supplies and trade beyond the Gulf. The Pentagon on Monday identified two US soldiers who were killed when Iran attacked an American base in Jordan on Friday, while they also said they had found unidentified remains that could be those of a third soldier reported as missing in action. Iran's Foreign Ministry said mediators had presented "proposals" to Teheran, signalling that diplomatic contacts remain active, but gave no details.

items.title: SpaceX to report first earnings as public company on Aug 4
items.summary: NEW YORK: SpaceX will release its first quarterly earnings report as a publicly traded company on Aug 4, the rocket and artificial intelligence (AI) giant said Monday on its investor relations website. SpaceX went public on June 12 at US$135 a share, with the stock later soaring as high as US$225.64 and pushing the company's valuation above US$2.6 trillion. Shares have since tumbled, closing at US$119.85 on Monday -- more than 45 per cent below their post-initial public offering (IPO) peak.

items.title: MoF: RON95 subsidy savings channelled to targeted aid, food security measures
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 21 — Savings generated from the targeted RON95 petrol subsidy mechanism are not being retained solely as a fiscal surplus, the Ministry of Finance (MOF) said. The ministry said Phase 1 of the e-invoice began on August 1, 2024, involving large companies with annual sales exceeding RM100 million. This was followed by Phase 2 for companies with annual sales of more than RM25 million up to RM100 million, Phase 3 for those with annual sales of more than RM5 million up to RM25 million, and Phase 4 for businesses with annual sales of up to RM5 million.

items.title: Beaten-down Indonesian stocks feel the love as AI rally cools
items.summary: SINGAPORE: As the rally in artificial intelligence (AI) stocks starts showing signs of fatigue and big investors turn sellers, some are buying back laggards and even warming to Indonesia's battered and unloved stock market. "We've been taking some profit in South Korea and buying Indonesia," said David Chao, Asia-Pacific global market strategist at Invesco in Singapore. "Indonesia is still probably the most under-looked macro growth story play."

items.title: Heat and World Cup hurt UK property asking prices
items.summary: LONDON: Asking prices for newly advertised British houses and apartments recorded an unusually large fall in the four weeks to July 11, as the men's soccer World Cup and record temperatures distracted buyers who also face high borrowing costs, property website Rightmove said on Monday. Prices for property put on sale dropped by 1.0 per cent, compared with the previous month, bigger than the average 0.2 per cent fall for the period over the past 10 years. Rightmove also said: * Asking ⁠prices were 0.4 per cent lower than a year earlier, after a 0.5 per cent annual drop in June * The average two-year fixed mortgage interest rate fell to 4.92 per cent from 5.07 per cent in June but was up from 4.25 per cent in February * Sales agreed in the first half of 2026 were 6.0 per cent ⁠below their level a year earlier but were similar to volumes in the first six months of 2024 * The number of homes available for sale fell 1.0 per cent compared to a year earlier but was close to a 12-year high for the time of year © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Loke: Senawang–Port Klang rail bypass to spur Negri economy
items.summary: SEREMBAN: The proposed construction of the Senawang–Port Klang bypass rail line, included in the Pakatan Harapan manifesto for the Negri Sembilan election, is expected to serve as a new catalyst for economic development in the southern part of the state. "When we plan to build this bypass rail line from Senawang to Port Klang, it will shorten the distance and make cargo transportation more efficient. Among the announcements were five high-impact projects, including the Senawang–Port Klang bypass rail line, a new Port Dickson International Port, a Transit-Oriented Development (TOD) project, an increase in two-way Seremban–Kuala Lumpur commuter rail services, and the construction of a new hospital in Bandar Enstek, Nilai.

items.title: South Korea's exports jump 52pct in first 20 days of July
items.summary: SEOUL: South Korea's exports for the first 20 days of July jumped 52.3 per cent from a year earlier, early data showed on Tuesday. Exports of semiconductors soared 180.6 per cent, while car exports declined 10.6 per cent, according to the government data. Destination wise, shipments to China jumped 94.1 per cent in July from a year earlier and exports to the US increased 39.6 per cent.

items.title: US judge approves Anthropic's US$1.5bil settlement of copyright lawsuit
items.summary: SAN FRANCISCO: A federal judge in San Francisco on Monday signed off on artificial intelligence company Anthropic's landmark US$1.5 billion settlement of a class action lawsuit brought by a group of authors who accused it of misusing their books to train its artificial intelligence (AI) chatbot Claude. US District Judge Araceli Martinez-Olguin granted final approval of the settlement, the largest known settlement of a US copyright case, rejecting arguments that it was too small. The authors' lead attorney, Justin Nelson, welcomed what he called a "historic settlement."

items.title: Three die after chicken lorry crashes into parked trailer
items.summary: TANAH MERAH: Three people, including a 16-year-old, were killed after their lorry crashed into a parked trailer along the East-West Highway here early today. He said 12 firemen from the Kemahang and Tanah Merah fire stations were deployed to the scene. "The three victims were trapped inside the lorry.

items.title: Foreigner nabbed after walking naked on Klang rooftop
items.summary: South Klang police chief Assistant Commissioner Lim Jit Huey said members of the public spotted the suspect at 7am before calling the Fire and Rescue Department to help lower him safely. "The South Klang Criminal Investigation Department has opened an investigation paper under Section 427 of the Penal Code for committing mischief and Section 377D of the Penal Code for gross indecency. Lim said Section 427 of the Penal Code carries a jail term of up to five years, a fine, or both, while Section 377D provides for a maximum sentence of two years' imprisonment.

items.title: Unions back PM's promotion review, seek focus on merit and enforcement incentives
items.summary: National Union of the Teaching Profession (NUTP) secretary-general Fouzi Singon said the union supports the move to streamline career progression, particularly by further empowering the "Excellent Teacher" incentive. He said it allows deserving teachers to bypass the usual eight-year wait altogether. He said NUTP has not seen evidence of young teachers quitting over slow promotions or pay concerns.

items.title: Saudi condemns Houthi maritime blockade threat
items.summary: RIYADH: Saudi Arabia on Monday condemned what it described as allegations by Yemen's Iran-aligned Houthis that the kingdom was besieging the Yemeni people, after the group announced it would impose a maritime blockade on Saudi Arabia. The Saudi foreign ministry condemned allegations by the Houthi military spokesperson that the kingdom was besieging Yemen and imposing a maritime blockade. Saudi would take "all necessary measures" to protect its ships, the ministry said in a statement.

items.title: New UK PM Burnham stamps authority with cabinet shakeup
items.summary: LONDON: New UK Prime Minister Andy Burnham took power Monday, purging the cabinet of his predecessor's loyalists as he promised a 10-year plan to ease cost-of-living pressures and signalled a tilt to the left. Burnham, 56, pledged to re-industrialise Britain and decentralise power as well as end sleeping rough on the streets after he met King Charles III at Buckingham Palace to become the head of government following Starmer's departure. In his first call with Trump, Burnham underlined his "commitment to defence and security", a UK government spokesperson said.

items.title: Human smuggling cases up 18pc in Kedah
items.summary: "For human smuggling cases, in 2025, police recorded 216 arrests involving 36 transporters and 180 migrants," he said. "This year, 256 individuals were arrested, comprising 30 transporters and 226 migrants. For the period January to June 2025, Kedah police recorded 11 arrests involving drug distribution cases.

items.title: Fuel smugglers exploit residents' unused subsidy quotas in Perlis
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Syndicates involved in subsidised fuel smuggling in Perlis have shifted tactics to exploit the identity cards of legitimate users who do not fully utilise their fuel quota. "Fuel is usually accumulated through repeated refuelling before being consolidated at illegal storage sites for further distribution and smuggling across the border," he said. Of the total, he said three cases involved raids on illegal fuel storage sites used by syndicates to accumulate fuel before distribution.

items.title: Syndicates tap locals with 'clean profiles' to move contraband
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Cross-border smuggling syndicates are exploiting border residents by recruiting Malaysians to play key operational roles — ranging from transporters and lookouts to logistics facilitators — in a bid to reduce the risk of detection along Malaysia's northern border. He said those living along the border were frequently hired to transport contraband or migrants to transit houses, where their less suspicious backgrounds allow them to bypass enforcement scrutiny. "The border folk are often hired to transport goods or migrants from the border to transit houses because they have clean profiles and locally registered vehicles that are less likely to raise suspicion."

items.title: N. Korea reaffirms support for Moscow's 'Ukrainian dispute'
items.summary: SEOUL: North Korea reaffirmed its support for Moscow's war on Ukraine as the two foreign ministers held talks in Moscow, Pyongyang's state media reported Tuesday. Russian President Vladimir Putin met the visiting diplomat on Sunday to thank North Korea for its support, which has included soldiers. North Korea has sent troops to Russia's Kursk region and supplied weapons systems to support Moscow's war effort.

items.title: Azam denies role in RM178mil deal won by brother's firm [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Former Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki has distanced himself from the RM178.29 million contract awarded to a company linked to his brother, saying he has no knowledge of the matter. Responding to an article published by The Edge Malaysia , Azam said his brother's business affairs had nothing to do with him. The report said Integriti Padu's executive chairman is Datuk Nasir Baki, Azam's brother.

items.title: Why is my TNB electricity bill suddenly high? The AFA fuel surcharge explained
items.summary: What is the AFA? The AFA is a monthly surcharge shown on your TNB bill that reflects changes in the cost of fuel and related generation expenses. TNB projects fuel and FX costs and sets an AFA projection for billing.

items.title: Semporna initiative nets 15 tonnes of marine waste
items.summary: MORE than 15 tonnes of floating solid waste have been removed from Semporna waters through the Puspabumi Eco Pledge Programme. Reef Check Malaysia senior programme manager Adzmin Fatta said the initiative was launched following reports of rubbish-filled plastic bags drifting in open waters. The programme, launched last November, is a joint initiative between Reef Check Malaysia and Puspabumi to promote sustainable practices among local tourism operators.

items.title: Trump orders 50pct tariff on many Canadian goods
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump signed orders Monday to impose new 50-per cent tariffs on many Canadian goods, claiming "discriminatory treatment" by Ottawa against American alcohol, automobile and dairy products. The latest duties will not apply to energy, potash and goods already impacted by sector-specific tariffs, the White House said. The White House, in announcing the new tariffs, said Canada was one of only two countries – along with China – to retaliate against Trump's sweeping tariffs last year.

items.title: RTM plans to air more global sports events
items.summary: RADIO Televisyen Malaysia (RTM) will announce the acquisition of broadcasting rights for more global sporting events after its successful free coverage of the 2026 World Cup, said Datuk Seri Fahmi Fadzil. The communications minister said broadcasting director-general Ashwad Ismail had informed him that RTM would be airing more tournaments. "That will be announced later," he said after attending the Unity Merdeka x RTM World Cup 2026 programme at Dataran Merdeka here early yesterday.

items.title: Penang Port key logistics gateway to Thai border
items.summary: JELEBU: Penang Port continues to play an important role as a logistics gateway for southern Thailand by handling container and cargo movements between the neighbouring country and Malaysia, said Transport Minister Anthony Loke. He said the port is handling a large number of containers to and from southern Thailand, thus strengthening its role as a regional logistics hub. "We do handle a lot of containers to and from Thailand, so Penang Port is now a gateway to southern Thailand.

items.title: 'Ukraine fired 400 drones at Moscow'
items.summary: MOST of the over 400 drones that targeted the Moscow region overnight were intercepted, but at least two people were wounded, said Russian authorities yesterday. Domodedovo Hospital also reported yesterday that six people, including three foreigners, were hospitalised with blast wounds in the Moscow region. Meanwhile, Ukraine said Russian missile strikes on a Turkish-owned cargo ship carrying grain had killed six people, hours after Moscow fired a fresh barrage of missiles at Kyiv.

items.title: Fahmi: PH has no shortage of Malay-Muslim leaders
items.summary: KUALA PILAH: Pakatan Harapan (PH) communications director Datuk Seri Fahmi Fadzil has dismissed claims that the federal government is neglecting Malay sensitivities. Responding to former Umno Youth chief Khairy Jamaluddin's assertion that Barisan Nasional's (BN) cooperation with Perikatan Nasional (PN) in the Negri Sembilan election was intended to signal the need for PH to pay greater attention to Malay concerns, Fahmi said Khairy was not the only person who understood such issues. My late grandfather and grandmother were Umno members.

items.title: Talks start on UN treaty to protect the elderly as life expectancy rises
items.summary: A UNITED Nations meeting last week heard calls for an end to ageism and better protection against what campaigners say are hidden abuses as negotiations began on a treaty to strengthen the rights of older people. "There are many situations where people are not fully protected by existing law," said Heidrun Mollenkopf, president of AGE Platform Europe, a network of older people. "There are age restrictions that go completely unchallenged," said Bridget Sleap, a senior researcher at Human Rights Watch, a rights watchdog.

items.title: Khalil al-Hayya: Gaza negotiator who will now lead Hamas
items.summary: FOR months, veteran Hamas leader Khalil al-Hayya spearheaded the group's indirect ceasefire negotiations with arch-foe Israel in an effort to halt the war in Gaza. "Khalil al-Hayya was elected by an overwhelming majority," a senior Hamas official told AFP, adding that he is expected to formally assume the role next week. The official highlighted Hayya's pragmatic and conversative temperament.

items.title: Lebanon museum depicts destruction of homes
items.summary: TEARS streamed down south Lebanon resident Fatima Hajj Ali's face as she stared at a host of keys hanging like windchimes from the ceiling of a Beirut museum — each one symbolising a home, like her own, destroyed by Israel. "Half the house collapsed and half remained," she said. he said.

items.title: Hamas names Khalil Al-Hayya as new overall leader
items.summary: Hayya replaces Yahya Sinwar, who was killed in combat with Israeli forces in October 2024 as Hamas fought a full-scale, two-year war with Israel in the Gaza Strip. Hayya, the group's exiled Gaza chief and top negotiator, has become an increasingly central figure in the leadership of Hamas since the deaths of Sinwar and Ismail Haniyeh, who was killed in Iran in July 2024. Hayya was among top Hamas officials targeted in 2025 in an Israeli attack in Doha, where he is based, Israeli officials told Reuters.

items.title: Tamim Dahri re-arrested after two-day remand; to be charged tomorrow
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, July 20 — Police re-arrested activist Tamim Dahri Abdul Razak after his two-day remand ended today, following his detention in Port Dickson. Langkawi police chief ACP Khairul Azhar Nuruddin said the 33-year-old was re-arrested by the Langkawi district police headquarters (IPD) Criminal Investigation Division in connection with a case being investigated under Section 295 of the Penal Code. “Immediately after the remand period ended, the suspect was re-arrested in connection with a police report lodged in Kuah, which is being investigated under that section.

items.title: EC dispatches 13,263 postal ballots for Negeri Sembilan state polls
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 20 — The Election Commission (EC) today issued 13,263 postal ballot papers to eligible voters for the 16th Negeri Sembilan state election. He said 12,669 postal ballots were issued under Form 1A, covering election officials, EC members and officers, police personnel, military personnel and media practitioners. Khairul Shahril reminded all postal voters to mark their ballot papers and complete the Identity Declaration Form (Form 2) accurately and in full.

items.title: AGC orders court inquiry into Durian Tunggal police shooting
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — The Attorney General's Chambers (AGC) has ordered a formal inquest into the deaths of three men fatally shot during a police operation in Durian Tunggal, Melaka, on November 24, 2025. The decision follows a comprehensive review of investigation papers submitted by the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM). “The Attorney General's Chambers takes seriously every incident involving the loss of life.

items.title: Premier hails AirBorneo’s RM374.88 fare, warns: raise it and you’ll face trouble
items.summary: KUCHING, July 20 — The ticket prices offered by AirBorneo Airways (AirBorneo) serve as a benchmark for other airlines, helping to stabilise airfares in the market, said Premier Datuk Patinggi Tan Sri Abang Johari Tun Openg. He said when other airlines saw the fares offered by the state-owned aviation company, they would be compelled to lower their own ticket prices in order to remain competitive. “We are at the mercy of these other airlines because they do not have Sarawak at heart,” he said when launching AirBorneo’s inaugural Kuching (KCH)–Kuala Lumpur (KUL) flight at Kuching International Airport today.

items.title: Aminuddin urges integrity in Negeri Sembilan polls, condemns vandalism of campaign materials
items.summary: PORT DICKSON, July 20 — Negeri Sembilan Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun has called on all political parties to conduct their campaigns with integrity and in full compliance with election laws and regulations throughout the 16th Negeri Sembilan State Election. He was commenting on a statement issued earlier today by the PH Media Centre Secretariat for the 16th Negeri Sembilan State Election, which said the coalition’s main operations centre had received several reports of vandalism involving campaign materials on the second day of the campaign. For the latest updates on the 16th Negeri Sembilan State Election, visit https://prn.bernama.com/n9.

items.title: Zahid warns of fraud surge, says Cyber Crime Bill 2026 will fortify Malaysia’s defences
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — A total of 8,014 charges related to online fraud crimes have been recorded as of May this year, surpassing the total of 6,140 charges throughout 2025, said Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi. He said the statistics showed that the threat of online fraud crimes was becoming more serious not only in terms of the increasing number of cases, but also the increasing financial losses to the people. Ahmad Zahid said the arrest statistics showed the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM)’s continued action in combating online fraud crimes.

items.title: Trailer crash in Sik kills van driver, injures two including former Kedah assemblyman’s wife
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — A man was killed while two others were injured in a three-vehicle crash involving a trailer lorry at Batu 34, Jalan Kuala Sin, Sik, Kedah, today. “Upon arrival at the scene, we found a road accident involving a container trailer lorry, a Nissan X-Trail SUV and a Toyota Hiace van,” he said in a statement today. The trailer lorry driver was not injured,” he said.

items.title: Sabah viral school assault: Six students charged with rioting, claim trial
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — Six female students aged between 12 and 16 were charged in the Court for Children in Kota Kinabalu today with rioting over an alleged assault on a 13-year-old student outside a secondary school that was captured in a video which later went viral. The New Straits Times previously reported that the incident drew public attention after a video showing a group of students allegedly assaulting a Form One student circulated widely on social media on May 27. The six are represented by Datuk Ram Singh and Prabjit Singh Gill, while Deputy Public Prosecutor Arfa Anchal Khan Muhammad Munir Khan appeared for the prosecution.

items.title: Oil palm lorry driver killed after being pinned in cab in Jasin crash
items.summary: Berita Harian reported that the victim was pronounced dead at the scene by Health Ministry (MOH) personnel after he was found trapped in the driver’s cabin of the lorry. Melaka Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) Public Relations Officer Mohd Hafidzatullah Rashid said the department received an emergency call regarding the incident at 11.50am. He said six firefighters from the Merlimau Fire and Rescue Station were dispatched to the scene with a Light Fire Rescue Tender (LFRT) vehicle and an Emergency Medical Rescue Service (EMRS) unit.

items.title: Pet project in Parliament: Government weighs fund and tax breaks for animal welfare
items.summary: Political secretary at the Prime Minister’s Office (PMO) Datuk Azman Abidin said that the lack of a special budget has forced most NGOs and animal shelters to operate voluntarily, using their own funds to manage and rescue stray animals. It requires the involvement of all parties, including government agencies, local authorities, NGOs, veterinarians, industry players, academia, and the community,” he said. Also present at the dialogue session were the Malaysian Animal Association deputy director-general Dr Aida Muhid, Malaysian Animal Association president Dr Arie Dwi Andika, and Department of Environmental Health director Dr Nor Halizam binti Ismail.

items.title: Prison walls get digital armour: Drone smuggling bid triggers zero‑tolerance tech blitz
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — The Malaysian Prison Department will enhance security measures, including using technology, intelligence monitoring and patrols, to counter the growing threat posed by drones being used for criminal activities. The proactive measures follow the department’s detection and successful prevention of an attempt to smuggle contraband into the Sungai Udang Prison Complex using a drone on July 15. “Through monitoring and security control measures by the prison, the suspicious activity was detected, and a police report was lodged, enabling enforcement action to be taken.

items.title: Johor cops nab 12 men, including teen, after brawl breaks out during World Cup final screening in JB
items.summary: Johor police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said authorities received information regarding the incident at 5.40am. He said police also detected a viral video clip of the alleged incident that was uploaded on a Facebook group. On the arrests, Ab Rahaman said three of the suspects had records for previous criminal cases.

items.title: Man killed in suspected hit-and-run in Seremban while putting up party flags in N. Sembilan
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — A man was killed in a suspected hit-and-run incident while putting up party flags along Jalan Kuala Sawah-Rantau near Kampung Belangkan in Seremban on Sunday evening. The incident, which occurred at about 6.40pm, involved a lorry and the 23-year-old victim, according to a report by Sinar Harian . Seremban district police chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Yatim Osman said preliminary investigations found that the victim was on the left shoulder of the road heading towards Rantau while carrying out work to install party flags.

items.title: Hannah Yeoh blasts litterbugs, praises DBKL for extra‑time clean‑up
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh has expressed appreciation to Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) workers who worked tirelessly to clean up Dataran Merdeka after the area was littered with rubbish following the public screening of the 2026 Fifa World Cup final. Hannah also urged the public to play their part in keeping public spaces clean, saying disposing of rubbish in bins is a basic civic responsibility that should not be left entirely to public workers. “Yes, I know every taxpayer deserves good public services.

items.title: Public nabs man after attempted house break-in in Johor Bahru
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 20 — A man’s attempt to trespass into a house in Kampung Melayu Majidee here was foiled after members of the public detained him before handing him over to police this morning. Johor Bahru South district police chief Assistant Commissioner Azrul Hisham Mohd Shaffei said police received a report at 8.42am from a 33-year-old local man, who is also a police officer. Following the report, officers from the Majidee police station went to the scene and arrested the suspect at about 9am.

items.title: Kelantan police station chief remanded seven days as MACC probes alleged RM500 bribe
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, July 20 — A police station chief has been remanded for seven days to assist investigations into allegations that he accepted a RM500 bribe in exchange for not taking action against a foreign national. The 41-year-old suspect, who holds the rank of sergeant major, arrived at the Kota Bharu Court Complex at about 9.05am wearing the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) lock-up attire and was escorted by two MACC officers. Kelantan MACC director Azmin Yusoff confirmed that the suspect was arrested at about 2.20pm yesterday.

items.title: Kelantan labourer fined RM3,000 after toddler found with meth, benzodiazepines in system
items.summary: PASIR MAS, July 20 — A labourer was fined RM3,000, placed on a good behaviour bond and ordered to perform 36 hours of community service after pleading guilty to exposing his one-year-eight-month-old daughter to dangerous drugs two months ago. Muhammad Shahrul Hisham Lailatulazhma, 27, entered the guilty plea before Sessions Court Judge Zulkpli Abdullah, Kosmo! The accused paid both the fine and the good behaviour bond ordered by the court.

items.title: Sarawak’s jet age begins as Abang Johari joins AirBorneo maiden lift‑off to Kuala Lumpur
items.summary: KUCHING, July 20 — AirBorneo today made history with the commencement of its first jet operations through the inaugural Kuching-Kuala Lumpur flight. Sarawak Premier Tan Sri Abang Johari Tun Openg was among the passengers on the maiden flight, MY5005, which departed from Kuching International Airport at 1.20pm for Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA). Like Sarawak, Japan is an island so connectivity is extremely important.

items.title: Sessions Court hears RM8m FashionValet transfer, Vivy Yusof and husband deny breach of trust
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — The Sessions Court here was told today that RM8 million was transferred from FashionValet Sdn Bhd’s bank account to 30 Maple Sdn Bhd through two online transactions in August 2018. CIMB Bank’s Taman Tun Dr Ismail branch Customer Service Manager Azizan Hassan, 45, testified that a review of 30 Maple’s bank statements revealed online deposits of RM5 million and RM3 million from FashionValet’s bank account. “On August 21, 2018, there was a deposit of RM5 million from FashionValet Sdn Bhd with the transaction description, ‘REMITTANCE CR/ROC/PB ECP PAYMENT FASHIONVALET SDN BHD PB ECP PAYMENT FASHION VALET SDN BHD ADVANCES TO 30 MAPLE’.

items.title: Sabah pockets RM600m, presses Putrajaya for RM900m balance under interim deal
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, July 20 — Sabah has received RM600 million of the RM1.5 billion interim special grant from the federal government, with the balance of RM900 million requested to be paid within the year, said state Assistant Finance Minister Datuk Mohd Ishak Ayub. He said the RM1.5 billion payment announced by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim is an interim arrangement and does not represent the final amount due to Sabah under the Federal Constitution. Other allocations include RM278 million for operational expenditure, RM210 million for investments, RM162 million for administrative expenses, RM93 million for domestic grants and RM13 million under special provisions.

items.title: Lab tests confirm Selangor ICU patient free of leptospirosis, health dept dismisses viral claims
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, July 20 — Laboratory tests have confirmed that a patient admitted to the Intensive Care Unit (ICU) of a hospital in Selangor on July 7 with suspected leptospirosis did not contract the disease, the Selangor State Health Department (JKNS) said today. “Of the total, 26 cases, or 21 per cent, were linked to recreational water activities,” said the statement, which was issued following the circulation of social media messages claiming a leptospirosis case had originated from a recreational water area in Selangor. It said three recreational water sites in Selangor frequently associated with leptospirosis cases are Taman Eko Rimba Sungai Kanching in Gombak, Sungai Congkak Waterfall in Hulu Langat and Sungai Ulu Yam in Hulu Selangor.

items.title: Malaysia welcomes Zhouzhuang partnership, pledges stronger cultural ties and heritage conservation with China
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — Malaysia today welcomed a tourism and heritage cooperation initiative with China’s century-old Zhouzhuang Ancient Town, aimed at strengthening cultural exchange, sustainable tourism and heritage conservation between both countries. Ye Xin, general manager of Kunshan Water Town Zhouzhuang Cultural Tourism Development Group Co. Limited, said China and Malaysia share a common cultural heritage and a long history of cultural exchanges. “Malaysia’s thriving tourism industry and abundant outbound travel market make it a key overseas market for Zhouzhuang.

items.title: Immigration officer pleads not guilty to seven bribery charges totalling RM1,950
items.summary: MELAKA, July 20 — A Melaka Immigration Department officer pleaded not guilty at the Ayer Keroh Sessions Court here today to seven charges of accepting bribes totalling RM1,950 last year. Mohd Nazrul Md Yasin, 39, entered the plea before Judge Mohamad Kamil Nizam. The foreign national, identified as Kusmiyati, was allegedly staying at the premises, an offence under the Immigration Act 1959/63.

items.title: Sweeping storm alert issued, MetMalaysia says treacherous weather threatens peninsula and Sarawak
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 20 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds expected to hit several states in Peninsular Malaysia and Sarawak until 5pm today. MetMalaysia announced that the affected areas are Kuala Lumpur, Perlis, Penang, Melaka as well as in Kedah involving the areas of Kubang Pasu, Kota Setar, Pokok Sena, Padang Terap, Kulim and Bandar Baharu as well as Perak in Manjung, Central Perak, Bagan Datuk, Hilir Perak and Muallim. Other states also affected are Negeri Sembilan in Jelebu, Kuala Pilah, Jempol and Tampin as well as Johor in Tangkak, Muar, Batu Pahat and Johor Bahru.
